data.title: Huntington Bancshares GAAP EPS of $0.34 beats by $0.03, revenue of $1.94B beats by $50M
data.description:
• Huntington Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:HBAN): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.34 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $1.94B (+9.6% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • Net interest income increased $31 million, or 2%, from the prior quarter, and $139 million, or 11%, from the year-ago quarter.
    •Total deposit costs were 2.03%, down 13 basis points from the prior quarter.
  • Average total loans and leases increased $2.7 billion, or 2%, from the prior quarter to $130.9 billion, and increased $8.9 billion, or 7%, from the year-ago quarter.
  • Average total deposits increased $2.2 billion, or 1%, from the prior quarter and $10.9 billion, or 7%, from the year-ago quarter.
  • Allowance for credit losses (ACL) of $2.5 billion, or 1.87% of total loans and leases, at quarter end, an increase of $32 million from the prior quarter.
  • Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) risk-based capital ratio was 10.6%, at March 31, 2025, up from 10.5% in the prior quarter. Adjusted Common Equity Tier 1, including the effect of AOCI, was 8.9%, up from 8.7% in the prior quarter.
  • Tangible common equity (TCE) ratio of 6.3%, up from 6.1% in the prior quarter and 6.0% from a year ago.
  • Tangible book value per share of $8.80, up $0.47, or 6%, from the prior quarter and up $1.03, or 13%, from a year ago.
  • The Board of Directors approved a $1 billion share repurchase authorization.

data.title: Tilray Brands proposes reverse stock split
data.description:
Tilray Brands (NASDAQ:TLRY) has proposed a reverse stock split of the company’s common stock at a ratio ranging from 1-to-10 to 1-to-20.

The exact ratio will be determined by the board of directors, without reducing the authorized number of shares of our common stock. CEO Irwin Simon said, “The Reverse Stock Split will better align Tilray’s number of shares outstanding with companies of our size and scope. A higher price per share would ensure compliance with Nasdaq's continued listing requirements and places Tilray in a position to continue executing on our strategic plans.”

As of quarter ended February 28, 2025, Tilray’s balance sheet had a cash and marketable securities balance of over $248M. During the fiscal year 2025 to date, the company reduced its total debt outstanding by around $76M.

Shares fell 5.8% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: SpaceX, Palantir, Anduril emerge as frontrunners for Trump’s “Golden Dome” project - report
data.description:
Elon Musk’s space technology company SpaceX (SPACE) has surfaced as a leading contender to develop critical components for President Donald Trump’s ambitious ‘Golden Dome’ missile defense shield, Reuters reported, citing sources.

Trump in March announced plans for the Golden Dome, similar to Israel's Iron Dome air defense system, designed to protect against inbound missile attacks. The Office of the Undersecretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment was working with partner agencies to make that happen, the department of defense said at the time.

SpaceX is teaming up with software firm Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) and drone manufacturer Anduril to bid on developing critical components of the Golden Dome project, Reuters reported citing six sources said. The three companies have reportedly met with senior Trump administration officials and Pentagon leaders recently to pitch their plan.

The proposal includes deploying a constellation of 400 to over 1,000 satellites to detect and track incoming missiles around the globe, the sources said. In a second phase, a separate fleet of roughly 200 attack satellites, armed with missiles or lasers, would be used to intercept and neutralize threats, according to three of the sources. The SpaceX-led group is not expected to be involved in arming the satellites.

SpaceX has also proposed structuring its involvement in the Golden Dome project as a "subscription service," where the government would pay for access to the technology instead of owning the system outright. The subscription model could bypass certain Pentagon procurement procedures, potentially enabling a faster rollout of the system, according to two sources.

The White House, SpaceX, Palantir, and Anduril did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha’s request for comment.

More than 180 companies have expressed interest in contributing to the development and construction of the Golden Dome project, according to a U.S. official.

data.title: Eli Lilly jumps as oral GLP-1 drug succeeds in Phase 3 trial
data.description:
• Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) shares climbed ~14% in the premarket on Thursday after the company said its oral GLP-1 receptor agonist orforglipron reached the main goal in a Phase 3 trial for adults with type 2 diabetes.
  • Citing topline data from its ACHIEVE-1 clinical trial for a key secondary endpoint, the Indiana-based pharma giant said that the once-daily pill reduced weight by ~8% on average (16.0 lbs) at the highest dose in the 40-week trial.
  • LLY’s main rival in the weight loss drug market, Novo Nordisk (NVO), fell ~5% in reaction in the premarket, while other obesity drug developers such as Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VKTX) and Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) traded lower. However, Structure Therapeutics (GPCR) added ~8%.

This is a developing story. Check back for more updates.

data.title: Hertz Global continues to soar as analysts weigh the impact of Pershing Square
data.description:
Hertz Global (NASDAQ:HTZ) extended on its monster rally that was kicked off after Pershing Square disclosed that it acquired 4.1% of the car rental company in Q1. With the reported 19.8% stake, Bill Ackman's firm is the second-largest shareholder of Hertz (NASDAQ:HTZ) behind Knighthead Capital, which owns 58.9% of outstanding shares.

Bank of America analyst John Babcock highlighted that Pershing Square has launched activist campaigns in the past and could also be looking for an activist role at Hertz (HTZ). He thinks the activist investor could seek to push the board for changes in how Hertz (HZ) operates and request board representation.

Babcock said the Hertz (HTZ) rally could allow the company more flexibility to use the equity markets, which had been difficult to justify when the shares were trading meaningfully lower. He estimated that Hertz (HTZ) will need to raise more than $500 million in additional capital

In its assessment of Hertz Global, Morgan Stanley teased the AI case for the company. Analyst Adam Jonas noted the next chapters of autonomous vehicles requiring a robust collection of downstream fulfillment infrastructure required to maintain the efficient operation of the robotic megafleet. "Our discussions with tech enablers and OEMs over many years have hinted to the potential strategic value of traditional car rental assets/rooftops in an AI-enabled transport utility," he wrote.

Shares of Hertz Global (HTZ) were up 25.9% in premarket trading to a new 52-week high of $7.19 after spiking 56.4% on Wednesday. Short interest on the stock stands at 17.3% of the total float, which may be adding to trading volatility.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures mixed after tech led selloff
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed on Thursday, while yields gained, as Wall Street hoped for a rebound a day after a tech-driven selloff.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -1.23%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.32%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.82%.

Wall Street on Wednesday ended deep in the red after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell expressed concerns about the likely higher-than-anticipated effects from tariffs, including possibly putting pressure on the central bank's dual mandate of stable prices and strong employment.

"Ahead of the Easter weekend, risk assets sold off again, as ongoing trade tensions and hawkish comments from Fed Chair Powell led to growing concern about the near-term outlook, particularly if the Fed would be reluctant to ease policy," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"In terms of the latest on the trade front, investors were most concerned about chipmakers yesterday, which followed the new restrictions from the Donald Trump administration on semiconductor exports to China," Allen said.

On the economic front, eyes will be on the March housing starts and permits, initial jobless claims, and the April Philly Fed business outlook, all landing at the same time.

The initial jobless claims are expected to rise to 225K, and the Philly Fed business outlook is anticipated to come in at  2.2.

"The US Philly Fed business sentiment survey is due. All sentiment data is extremely suspect nowadays, but markets are focused on the corporate inactivity amidst policy uncertainty. Even unreliable evidence, therefore, has influence," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Markets will be closed tomorrow on account of Good Friday.
